description: This image displays three panels of remote sensing data, representative of Interferometric Synthetic Aperture Radar (InSAR) processing outputs. The leftmost panel is a grey-scale image, likely depicting radar amplitude, showing a land area with a distinct urban or developed core (lighter tones, complex geometric features) surrounded by darker, textured regions indicative of vegetation or natural landscapes. The middle and right panels are colour-coded interferograms, characteristic of wrapped interferometric phase. Both interferogram panels show speckled patterns with repeating horizontal bands of colour, cycling from red and orange through yellow and green to various shades of blue, set against a grey background. These colour patterns encode phase information, which is used to measure surface deformation or elevation changes. The specific phase patterns differ between the middle and right panels, suggesting distinct interferometric pairs or processing results. No text labels, geographic markers, scale bar, or temporal information is visible within the image.
